# nayatel_cube (Resource)

Manages a Nayatel Cube — a managed container (LXD-backed), distinct from IaaS virtual machines.

The CPU/RAM pair must be one of the combinations offered by Nayatel (e.g. 2/2, 2/4, 4/8). The provider validates this before creating.

## Import

Cubes can be imported using the cube instance name (`{name}-{username}`):

```
terraform import nayatel_cube.example <cube_instance_name>
```



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cpu` (Number) Number of CPU cores. Must form an offered combination with `ram`. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `image_version` (String) OS image version (e.g. `22.04`, `24.04`). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) Name of the cube. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `ram` (Number) RAM in GB. Must form an offered combination with `cpu`. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `ssh_public_key` (String) SSH public key for authentication. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `storage` (Number) Root disk size in GB. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the cube. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `floating_ips` (Number) Number of floating IPs. Defaults to 1. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `image_name` (String) OS image name. Defaults to `ubuntu`. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Cube instance identifier (`{name}-{username}`)
- `project_id` (String) Cube project identifier (e.g. `cubes-prj-<user>-<n>`)
- `public_ip` (String) Public IP address of the cube
- `status` (String) Current status of the cube (Running, Stopped, Error)
